title: Seeded Asteroid Generation, Polygons, Sizes, and Difficulty Inputs · Guided Program Studio
summary: A two-person studio incrementally builds seeded readable asteroid factory from original geometric fixtures while retaining earlier Python, repository, determinism, frame-budget, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of seeded readable asteroid factory while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness.
Generate ordered angular vertices with bounded radius variation and validate minimum size, winding, and intersections.
Define large, medium, and small radius, speed, score, fragment, rendering, and collision contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Seeded Rng
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 1556 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Seeded Rng.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Seeded Rng, keep Seeded Rng connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Difficulty Inputs
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-inputs-yix8c0" that uses case 3692 to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Difficulty Inputs.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Difficulty Inputs into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Difficulty Inputs, keep Difficulty Inputs connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-inputs-yix8c0
# Competency: asteroids-difficulty-inputs.
# Case 3692: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_difficulty_inputs_yix8c0(vertices: int = 11, min_radius: float = 18.0, max_radius: float = 31.0, seeded_equal: bool = True, self_intersections: int = 0, spawn_budget: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_yix8c0 = "yix8c0-0c8xiy-3692"
    assert evidence_variant_yix8c0
    evidence_axes_yix8c0 = (
        "playeryix8c0",
        "seed3692",
        "input0c8xiy",
        "frameyix8c03692",
        "state3692yix8c0",
        "playtest0c8xiy3692",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_yix8c0)) == 6
    if vertices < 5 or min_radius <= 0.0 or max_radius < min_radius:
        return False, vertices, "polygon-domain"
    if not seeded_equal:
        return False, vertices, "rng-drift"
    if self_intersections > 0:
        return False, self_intersections, "invalid-polygon"
    if spawn_budget < 1:
        return False, spawn_budget, "spawn-budget"
    assert max_radius >= min_radius
    return True, spawn_budget, "seeded-asteroids"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Difficulty Inputs
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 5508 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound count, speed, size, spawn interval, aim burden, cue clarity, and selectable assistance as separate difficulty inputs.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
